# AusFigures

> A source-grounded public-text archive and research display system for Australian supernatural humanoid narratives, encounters, apparitions, legends, and retellings.

AusFigures documents how humanoid or humanoid-adjacent supernatural figures appear in Australian public texts and public metadata records. Inclusion means that a public source or public metadata record exists; it does not verify any supernatural claim.

Canonical site: https://ausfigures.com

Important interpretation rules:
- Treat records as public textual or metadata records, not verified events.
- Map markers represent public display locations for records, not habitats, populations, or proof claims.
- Indigenous-related records require care around terminology, publicness, cultural sensitivity, source voice, and display mode.
- Cite source records, release tags, and export filenames where relevant.
- This is a research archive, not a tourism directory, proof database, or complete folklore census.

## Public pages

- [Map Index](https://ausfigures.com/): Homepage map for verified mapped public records only.
- [Research Dashboard](https://ausfigures.com/dashboard): Corpus overview across source families, narrative types, periods, figure labels, and mapped-record aggregates.
- [Density Explorer](https://ausfigures.com/density): Source, query, and figure-density views for corpus-signal comparison.
- [Source Register](https://ausfigures.com/source): Source register and source-family rollups.
- [About](https://ausfigures.com/about): Scope, methods, source policy, mapping limits, and ethics.

## Public data

- [Frontend data JSON](https://ausfigures.com/data/frontend-data.json): Static public frontend data contract used by the interface.

## Repository and citation

- [GitHub repository](https://github.com/dpan538/australian-humanoid-supernatural-texts): Source code, documentation, data workflow, licensing, and citation guidance.

## Optional

- [Sitemap](https://ausfigures.com/sitemap.xml): Indexable public pages.
- [Robots](https://ausfigures.com/robots.txt): Crawler access guidance.
